decision.scope: K-12 classroom use during regular school hours. Not approved for assessment or grading workflows.

rubric.description: Vendor processes student records in a FERPA-compliant manner.
Vendor obtains verifiable parental consent for users under 13.
Vendor does not use student-provided content for model training.
rubric.notes: Student Disclosure asserts no-training; district requires contractual confirmation.

rubric.description: Vendor filters out content inappropriate for the declared age range.
Vendor has completed a third-party bias audit within the last 18 months.
rubric.notes: Bias audit completed 2025-03; due for refresh by 2026-09 per Tutor Card.

rubric.description: Vendor has a documented escalation path for self-harm or abuse disclosures.

conditions.description: Vendor SHALL NOT use Springfield USD student-provided content for model training, fine-tuning, or evaluation. Inference logs SHALL be deleted within 30 days.
conditions.violation_response: Contract termination and breach notification to families within 60 days.

conditions.description: Vendor SHALL deliver a refreshed third-party bias audit no later than 2026-12-01. Continued approval is contingent on this delivery.
conditions.violation_response: Suspension of approval pending refreshed audit.

conditions.description: Vendor SHALL display a clear notice that AcmeTutor outputs MUST NOT be used for student assessment or grading.
conditions.violation_response: Immediate restriction of district account access pending remediation.

rationale: AcmeTutor 3.0 meets all hard requirements for FERPA, COPPA, and age-appropriate content filtering. The vendor's Student AI Disclosure already commits to no-training-on-student-data; the district requires contractual reaffirmation of this commitment. The bias audit is current but approaching its refresh window. Approval is granted for K-12 classroom use only, with explicit exclusion of assessment and grading workflows, contingent on the three conditions listed.

history.note: Approval contingent on three documented conditions.

signatures.signer: Dr. Jane Doe, Director of Educational Technology
